title: API key schema with SHA-256 hashing, key_prefix, role enum, and Cloak encryption

story.i_want_to: implement an ApiKey schema with SHA-256 hashed key storage, a key_prefix for identification, a role enum (superadmin, user, orchestrator, agent), expiration, revocation, and Cloak encryption for any sensitive fields at rest
story.so_that: the system has secure, non-reversible API key storage where raw keys are only visible once at creation, keys are identifiable by prefix without exposure, and access control is role-based

rationale: API keys are the sole authentication mechanism for loopctl. Security requires that raw keys are never stored - only their SHA-256 hash. The key_prefix (first 8 chars) allows users to identify which key is which without seeing the full key. Role assignment per key enables the two-tier trust model where agents and orchestrators have different write permissions. Cloak encryption adds defense-in-depth for any sensitive metadata.

acceptance_criteria.description: A Loopctl.Auth.ApiKey schema exists with fields: id, tenant_id, name, key_hash, key_prefix (8 chars), role (enum), agent_id, last_used_at, expires_at, revoked_at, inserted_at
Raw API keys are generated with a 'lc_' prefix followed by 40 random URL-safe base64 characters (total ~43 chars)
The key_hash field stores the SHA-256 hash of the raw key; the raw key is never persisted to the database
The key_prefix field stores the first 8 characters of the raw key (e.g., 'lc_a3Bf9') for identification
The role enum supports four values: :superadmin, :user, :orchestrator, :agent
A Loopctl.Auth context module provides: generate_api_key/1 (returns {raw_key, %ApiKey{}}), verify_api_key/1 (hashes input and looks up by key_hash), revoke_api_key/1
verify_api_key/1 rejects keys that are revoked (revoked_at not nil) or expired (expires_at < now)
verify_api_key/1 updates last_used_at timestamp on successful verification
tenant_id is NULL for superadmin keys and required for all other roles
The api_keys table has a unique index on key_hash and an index on tenant_id
Every mutation (create, update, delete) MUST be recorded in the audit log via Ecto.Multi in the same transaction as the entity change.

test_cases.name: Generate API key returns raw key and persisted record
expected_results: Returns {:ok, {raw_key, %ApiKey{}}}
raw_key starts with 'lc_'
raw_key is ~43 characters long
ApiKey.key_prefix matches the first 8 chars of raw_key
ApiKey.key_hash is a SHA-256 hex digest (64 chars)
The raw_key is NOT stored in any field of the ApiKey record

test_cases.name: Verify API key with valid raw key
expected_results: Returns {:ok, %ApiKey{}} with the matching record
The returned ApiKey has the correct tenant_id and role
last_used_at is updated to approximately now

test_cases.name: Verify API key rejects revoked key
expected_results: Returns {:error, :unauthorized}

test_cases.name: Verify API key rejects expired key
expected_results: Returns {:error, :unauthorized}

test_cases.name: Verify API key rejects unknown key
expected_results: Returns {:error, :unauthorized}

test_cases.name: Revoke API key sets revoked_at
expected_results: Returns {:ok, %ApiKey{}} with revoked_at set to approximately now
Subsequent verify_api_key with the raw key returns {:error, :unauthorized}

test_cases.name: Superadmin key has nil tenant_id
expected_results: Returns {:ok, {raw_key, %ApiKey{tenant_id: nil, role: :superadmin}}}

test_cases.name: Non-superadmin key requires tenant_id
expected_results: Returns {:error, %Ecto.Changeset{}} with error on tenant_id

test_cases.name: Mutation creates audit log entry for API key operations
steps: POST /api/v1/api_keys with user API key and body {"name": "new-key", "role": "agent"}
DELETE /api/v1/api_keys/:id with user API key to revoke the created key
expected_results: Audit log entry created with entity_type='api_key', entity_id=new_api_key.id, action='created', actor_type='api_key', actor_id=api_key.id
Audit log entry created with entity_type='api_key', entity_id=revoked_api_key.id, action='revoked', actor_type='api_key', actor_id=api_key.id
Audit entries are in the same transaction as the entity changes (no orphaned entity without audit)

test_cases.name: Cross-tenant isolation: tenant A cannot access tenant B data
expected_results: Returns empty list or 404, never tenant_a's data

technical_notes: The api_keys table needs RLS enabled with tenant_id policy, but superadmin keys (tenant_id IS NULL) need a special policy clause
Consider a separate changeset for creation (with key_hash, key_prefix) vs. update (only name, revoked_at, etc.)
The verify_api_key/1 function must be constant-time safe — use Plug.Crypto.secure_compare for hash comparison if applicable, though hash lookup by index is inherently timing-safe
Preload the tenant association on verify_api_key for use in the auth pipeline
Schema module MUST use `use Loopctl.Schema` (provides `@primary_key {:id, :binary_id, autogenerate: true}`, `@foreign_key_type :binary_id`, `@timestamps_opts [type: :utc_datetime_usec]`)
All context functions MUST return `{:ok, result}` or `{:error, reason}` tuples. Never raise exceptions for expected error cases.
Controller MUST use `action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController` for error rendering (maps error atoms to HTTP status codes per US-1.4)
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files

Initial implementation of tenant/API key creation may skip audit logging. Audit logging for these entities is added retroactively after US-3.1 is implemented. The audit AC is a requirement for the FINAL state, not necessarily the initial commit.
